etymology.description: semantic 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") + phonetic 寺(寺) (sì, "temple") -> bamboo strips for grading -> rank; grade -> borrowed for wait; await
etymology.interpretation: The bamboo strips, 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo"), were used for writing and thus associated with record-keeping and classification leading to the meaning of rank or grade. The character 寺(寺) (sì, "temple") contributes the phonetic sound. Over time, the character was borrowed to express 'wait' and 'await.'

examples.chinese: 我们在大门口等你(我們在大門口等你)。
examples.english: We are waiting for you at the gate.

examples.chinese: 请等一下(請等一下)，我马上回来(我馬上回來)！
examples.pinyin: Qǐng děng yīxià, wǒ mǎshàng huílái!
examples.english: Please wait a moment, I will be back soon!

examples.chinese: 他的学术水平很高，属于一流的等级(他的學術水平很高，屬於一流的等級)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xuéshù shuǐpíng hěn gāo, shǔyú yīliú de děngjí.
examples.english: His academic level is very high, belonging to the top rank.
